p: Welcome to the official documentation for Budget Buddy. Whether you are a student trying to stretch your allowance, a savvy shopper hunting for the best deals, or a community contributor helping us keep prices updated, you are in the right place.
We rely on a community-driven approach to make sure everyone has access to accurate, up-to-date pricing across local stores.
Just want to use the app? Head over to the live platform and start saving today: 👉 Budget Buddy Iligan City 👈
Want to run it locally? If you are a developer looking to tinker with the MERN stack codebase, getting started is easy. Clone the repository and spin up the development server:

c:
git clone https://github.com/KishonShrill/BudgetBuddy-IliganCity.git
cd budget-buddy
npm install
npm run dev

p: We built this platform to solve a simple problem: finding the best grocery prices shouldn't require visiting five different stores.
Real-Time Price Tracking: Instantly search for everyday items and compare prices across multiple locations (like Gaisano, Shoppe 24, etc.).
Community-Powered Data: Prices are sourced, verified, and updated by shoppers like you.
Smart Cart & Budgeting: Add items to your virtual cart, set your budget limit, and let the app warn you if you are going over budget.
Rank & Reward System: Build trust by submitting accurate prices and voting on the Community Hub to rank up to Moderator status.
For Shoppers:
A modern web browser.
A Google Account
(👇👇👇 only required if 👇👇👇)
you want to submit prices
vote on pending items
rank up your contribution score
or save your profile data.
(👆👆👆 only required if 👆👆👆)
For Developers:
Node.js (v18+)
A MongoDB Database (Local or Atlas)
Basic understanding of React, Express, and Tailwind CSS.
What is Budget Buddy? - Learn about our mission and how the platform works.
Contribution Guide - Find out how to submit prices, earn points, and rank up in the community.
FAQ - Got questions? We've got answers.
"Helping the community save, one price check at a time."
Budget Buddy is a localized web application and community tool designed to help people track, compare, and discover product prices across various stores in Iligan City.

Navigating different groceries, convenience stores, and local shops to find the best price takes time and effort. Budget Buddy solves this by bringing the prices directly to your screen, powered by the people who shop there every day.
Real-Time Price Tracking: Instantly search for everyday items and see where they are currently the cheapest.
Community-Sourced Data: Our database is kept alive by users who submit and verify prices as they shop.
Location-Based Searching: Filter products by specific stores (e.g., Shoppe 24, Gaisano) to see what's available near you.
Budget Calculator: Add items to your virtual cart to ensure you stay within your budget before you even step foot in the store.
By working together, we can create a more transparent and budget-friendly shopping experience for everyone in the city.
Budget Buddy thrives on accurate data. Every time you submit a new price or verify an existing one, you are actively helping other shoppers save money.
To keep the platform reliable, please follow these guidelines when adding to the Community Hub:
Check the Date: Ensure the price you are submitting is current. Do not submit prices from old receipts.
Accurate Locations: Double-check that you are attributing the price to the correct store branch.
Clear Photos: If uploading a product image or receipt proof, make sure the text and price tag are clearly legible.
Exact Names: Include the brand, product name, and size/weight (e.g., Energen Vanilla 30g rather than just Energen).
Every submission goes to the Community Hub under the "To Review" tab.
Upvotes (👍): Give an upvote if you have recently seen the item at that exact price in that store.
Downvotes (👎): Give a downvote if the price is outdated, the item is no longer stocked, or the details are incorrect.
Note: You have a limited number of votes and submissions per day to prevent spam.
Active and accurate contributors earn trust within the platform. As you build a track record of reliable submissions, you can rank up:
Regular User: Can submit prices and vote on pending items (up to the daily limit).
Moderator: Earned by consistently providing accurate data and identifying incorrect prices. Moderators have higher daily limits and can directly approve or reject community submissions.
Admin: Platform managers who oversee the entire database and user clearances.

l: Do I need an account to use Budget Buddy?
p: You can browse stores and view prices without an account. However, if you want to use the Cart feature, submit new prices, or vote on the Community Hub, you must log in or create an account.

l: How do I know the prices are accurate?
p: Prices are crowdsourced and verified by the community. A price must receive a specific number of upvotes (usually 10) in the Community Hub before it is officially published to the main store listings.

l: I saw a wrong price, what should I do?
p: If a published price is outdated, you can submit a new, updated price for that item. Once the community verifies your new submission, it will replace the old price.

l: Why can't I submit any more prices today?
p: To ensure quality control and prevent spam, regular users are limited to a certain number of price submissions and votes per day (e.g., 5 votes and 1 submission). Your limits will reset at midnight.

l: How can I become a Moderator?
p: Moderator status is granted to users who have a long history of accurate price submissions and helpful community votes. Keep contributing quality data, and the admin team will upgrade your clearance level!
If you are experiencing issues with your account, spotted a bug, or need help navigating the platform, we are here to help.
When reporting a technical bug or an issue with a specific listing, please provide as much detail as possible:
A brief description of the problem.
The store or product you were looking at.
Screenshots (if applicable).
If you are a developer looking to contribute to the codebase, check out our repository to report issues or submit pull requests.
